html.message: Content unchanged and no code modifications - previous visual comparison remains valid

output.reason: First design QA run with all browsers - performing full visual comparison despite content unchanged flag

compositionGate.notes: Overall composition matches Figma design. Layout structure is correct: section header with eyebrow, heading, and description in a two-column layout, followed by vertically stacked event cards. Each card has an image half (left) with date badge overlay and content half (right) with title, location, description, and CTA button. Dark background (charcoal #1f1f1d with texture pattern) is correctly applied via pseudo-element. Rounded corners (rounded-3xl = 24px, close to Figma's 25px) on cards match design intent.

output.crossBrowserConsistency: All three browsers (Chromium, Firefox, WebKit) render identically at all breakpoints. No browser-specific rendering issues detected.

measurements.note: Theme heading scale intentionally maps Figma sizes to responsive scale. text-header-3 is the correct semantic level for this heading. Visual proportion is appropriate.
Theme heading utilities use 1.2 line-height consistently. Minor visual difference, within tolerance for design system consistency.
Figma shows single card. Implementation stacks multiple cards with 24px gap which is visually appropriate.
Theme heading scale maps this level. Visually the day number is legible and proportionate within the badge.
Theme uses General Sans as its sans-serif font throughout. This is a design system decision - General Sans replaces Roboto for body text. Visually similar.
Theme heading scale maps H4 level. Visually the title is bold Anton uppercase and readable, fitting the card proportions.
Implementation adds uppercase via text-header-4 utility. This is consistent with the theme's heading convention.

General Sans Medium 16px/1.5 (font-sans font-medium text-base leading-[1.5])
measurements.note: Same design system font substitution as other body text.

measurements.figmaValue: Text button with arrow, uppercase, Roboto Medium 16px
measurements.implementationValue: btn-tertiary component, uppercase, with arrow icon

tablet_768.notes: At 768px, the event cards transition to the horizontal layout (md:flex-row) with image left and content right. The header switches to 2-column grid layout. Card height is enforced at 349px (md:h-[349px]). Date badge remains correctly positioned in top-right of image area. Visual proportions match the Figma desktop design at this breakpoint. Spacing and typography are consistent.

tablet_1024.notes: At 1024px, layout is identical to 1440px desktop but narrower. Cards maintain horizontal layout, proportions are correct. No overflow or clipping issues. Content half text wraps appropriately.

mobile_375.notes: At 375px mobile, cards stack vertically (flex-col). Image appears on top, content below. The card no longer has the fixed 349px height constraint (only applies at md+), allowing content to flow naturally. Date badge remains visible over the image. Header switches to single-column stacked layout. All text is readable and no overflow issues. Figma only provides a desktop reference, so mobile layout is evaluated for visual quality and responsive behavior only.

notes.element: Heading sizes (section heading, event title, date number)
notes.description: Figma raw values are significantly larger than implementation (56px vs 30px for section heading, 48px vs 24px for event title, 40px vs 24px for date number). This is NOT a bug -- the theme has a deliberate heading scale system (text-header-0 through text-header-6) that maps semantic heading levels to responsive sizes. The correct semantic level is being used in each case (text-header-3 for section heading, text-header-4 for event title, text-header-5 for date number). Visually the headings are proportionate and readable.
Theme heading utilities use line-height 1.2 consistently, while Figma specifies 1.1 for headings. This is a design system convention, not a per-block issue. The 0.1 difference is within tolerance.

Figma uses 'Roboto Medium' for body text (description, date labels), while the implementation uses 'General Sans Medium' (the theme's --font-sans). This is a deliberate design system choice -- General Sans is the theme's body font throughout. The visual appearance is similar.
Figma specifies 25px border-radius, implementation uses rounded-3xl (24px). 1px difference, within Tailwind tolerance.
